title: RFE: podman generate kube storage usability
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

I spent way longer than I should have being very confused why the kube yaml I generated from a podman pod wasn't working. Basically, the problem was that it doesn't do anything for storage (or didn't for me) but is not very clear that it isn't doing it. As a result, when I try to launch it in kubernetes, it doesn't work and kube isn't very clear what the problem is.   

At the simplest, a warning saying "we didn't do any storage stuff to back the volumes, so you need to!" would be a good start. What would be even nicer is to generate a second file that is like `my-podname-storage-example.yaml` that has a simple "well if you mounted it all locally PV/PVC, this would work" with the expectation that users would modify to their needs.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

follow: https://docs.ckan.org/en/2.9/maintaining/installing/install-from-docker-compose.html using podman-compose then generate the kube yaml

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman-compose --version
['podman', '--version', '']
using podman version: 4.1.1
podman-composer version  1.0.3
podman --version 
podman version 4.1.1
exit code: 0
```

comments.body: @langdon Could you submit an example kube.yaml where you would want the warning?

Yeah:

* input -> [`docker-compose.yml`](https://github.com/ckan/ckan/blob/master/contrib/docker/docker-compose.yml)
* what podman generated; mostly, includes edits to work with the volume manifest -> [`ckan-manifest.yml`](https://github.com/BU-Spark/infra-public-data-portal/blob/main/contrib/kubernetes/ckan-manifest.yml)
* what I needed to create -> [`ckan-volume-manifest.yml`](https://github.com/BU-Spark/infra-public-data-portal/blob/main/contrib/kubernetes/ckan-volume-manifest.yml)

basically, when podman makes `ckan-manifest.yml` a warning that the user still needs to create something like `ckan-volume-manifest.yml`. Please keep in mind that the examples ^^ may not work correctly that are still WIP.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@langdon we generate PVCs for volumes you have used when creating containers/pods with podman. However this is not done automatically when you run `podman generate kube` on a pod or container - to generate the PVC yamls you need to pass in the volume names as well `podman generate kube volName` and something like this will be generated for you

```
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  annotations:
    volume.podman.io/driver: local
  creationTimestamp: "2022-08-02T04:05:53Z"
  name: pvc-test
spec:
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
status: {}
```

Is this what you were looking for or is there something else you would like podman to support or warn about?

The `podman volume ls` command will show you all the volumes you currently have created with podman. Generate kube will also create a multi yaml file with the PVC and pod definition when you pass both to the generate kube command, e.g `podman generate kube volName ctrName`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@langdon @umohnani8 What is going on with this one?
Sorry, been very busy. Will review by EOW
@langdon please reopen this if the suggestion in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15000#issuecomment-1226743111 isn't what you were looking for.
